title: Smart Commit: Distributed predictor validation
original_text: Continued development by making distributed predictor validation explicit. I updated ml/__init__.py so the package exposes the common API: PredictionResult, SklearnCommitPredictor, and predict_commit_type. Then I added predictor tests in tests/test_predictor.py that verify the public ml package exports work, the distributed model artifacts load successfully, and the shipped model predicts the core examples: fix, feat, docs, refactor, test, and chore. I also updated Roadmap.md to show 61 tests. Verification passed: QT_QPA_PLATFORM=offscreen python3 -m unittest discover -s tests -v. Result: 61 tests ran, 61 passed. git diff --check passed, and tracked __pycache__ remains clean.
expected_subject: test(ml): add distributed predictor validation suite
expected_body_lines: - Expose PredictionResult and SklearnCommitPredictor via ml/__init__
- Verify successful loading of shipped model artifacts
- Validate predictions for core types: fix, feat, docs, refactor, test, and chore
- Update Roadmap.md to reflect expanded test coverage
- Validation: all 61 tests pass, git diff --check clean
expected_command: git commit -m "test(ml): add distributed predictor validation suite"
